App store
All your key tools in-sync with your meetings
Cal.com works with all apps already in your flow ensuring everything works perfectly together.
Get started
Explore apps
Wall of love
See why our users love Cal.com
Read the impact we've had from those who matter most - our customers.
Get started
Book a demo
FAQ
Frequently asked questions
These are some of our most frequently asked questions.
Talk to sales
Get started
What is Cal.com and how does it work as a scheduling app?
Cal.com is a scheduling app and meeting scheduling software used by over a million people to eliminate booking back-and-forth. You share a link, and Cal.com handles calendar syncing, timezone detection, reminders, and video calls through Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, and Cal Video. It works as a simple meeting scheduler for 1-on-1s or a full automated scheduling system with routing and workflows, making it both a flexible scheduling tool and a powerful online booking system.
What makes Cal.com different from other scheduling apps?
Most scheduling apps limit free users heavily and force them to upgrade to paid tiers. Cal.com’s free tier is robust and always free for individuals. As a scheduler, Cal.com offers exceptional value to users by integrating seamlessly with common workflow tools like Google Calendar, Zoom, and Stripe. It also works as a lead routing, distribution, and management tool for inbound-focused teams. Overall, Cal.com is built for flexibility and customization. As a meeting scheduler, it has a robust free tier and very affordable paid tiers. Rare among scheduling tools, it provides high-impact features like custom domain support via your own SMTP server. This is currently an upcoming feature. Here’s the
waitlist
.
How much does Cal.com cost and what's included in each plan?
Cal.com's free plan includes unlimited event types, calendar syncing, workflow automation, Stripe payments, and Cal Video, making it one of the most fully featured free scheduling tools on the market. For teams, the booking software scales across four tiers. Teams ($16/user/month) adds round-robin scheduling, shared availability, and team workflows, making it ideal scheduling software for small business. Organizations ($37/user/month) include org-wide admin controls and centralized management for larger companies using Cal.com’s scheduler as their meeting scheduling software. Enterprise offers custom pricing with priority support, dedicated database, and higher SLAs. This is crucial for industries that require a secure online booking system.
What are Cal.com's pricing plans, and is it good scheduling software for small businesses?
Cal.com is one of the best options for scheduling software for small business thanks to a free tier that includes unlimited event types, workflow automation, Stripe payments, and Cal Video, making it the most generous free scheduling tool available. Small businesses needing booking software with team features can upgrade to Teams at $16/user/month for round-robin scheduling and shared availability. It also works as an online booking system for small businesses with embeddable booking pages. Organizations ($37/user/month) adds admin controls for mid-size to large companies. Enterprise offers custom pricing with priority support, dedicated database, and higher SLAs. The enterprise plan features are crucial for industries that require a secure meeting scheduling software with a secure database.
Can Cal.com be used as scheduling software for Healthcare, Sales, Support, and B2B teams?
Yes. Cal.com adapts as scheduling software across industries and team structures. Healthcare practices use it as patient scheduling software and physician scheduling software with HIPAA compliance, SOC 2 Type II certification, and encrypted storage. Thanks to the flexibility of Cal.com it is widely used by sales teams across industries. Support teams use the round-robin scheduling feature of Cal.com to fairly distribute workload among team members. For B2B sales teams, Cal.com works as booking software with attribute-based routing, round-robin lead distribution, and CRM updates in Salesforce or HubSpot on every booking. It's a complete automated scheduling system, online booking system, and scheduler for any vertical.
Trusted by fast-growing companies around the world
Smarter, simpler scheduling
Get started
Talk to sales

Everything you could need for scheduling as an individual.

Trusted by fast-growing companies around the world
Feature breakdown
Compare our Free and Teams plans to see why Cal.com is the better choice.
Features
Individual
Scheduling as an individual.
Free forever
Try for free
Teams
Schedule as a team.
$12
per month/user
Save 25%
Try for free
Organizations
Schedule company-wide.
$28
per month/user
Save 25%
Try for free
Enterprise
Scheduling without limits.
Custom pricing
Talk to sales
Unlimited event types
Create as many booking templates as you need for different meeting types.
Unlimited calendar connections
Connect multiple calendars to sync availability and prevent double bookings.
Unlimited meetings
Accept unlimited bookings without restrictions on the number of meetings.
Multiple duration options
Offer different meeting lengths for the same event type (e.g. 15, 30, or 60 mins)
Set custom meeting locations
Define where meetings take place, such as Zoom, Google Meet, phone, or in person.
Control your meeting availability
Set specific days, hours, and availability rules for each event type.
Automatic timezone detection & conflict checking
Automatically adjust for attendee timezones and prevent scheduling conflicts.
Meeting cancellation
Allow organizer and attendees to cancel meetings using a secure link.
Meeting rescheduling
Allow organizer and attendees to reschedule without back-and-forth emails.
Meeting reassignment
Transfer a scheduled meeting to another team member when needed.
Advanced features
Booking calendar & list view
View and manage all your upcoming bookings in a calendar or list format.
Change booking questions
Customize the questions attendees answer when booking.
Requires confirmation
Review and approve booking requests before the meeting is officially scheduled.
Require host's email verification
Ensure attendees confirm their email before the booking is finalized.
Private links
Create invite-only booking links that aren’t publicly discoverable.
Offer seats
Allow multiple people to book the same time slot for group sessions or classes.
Hide organizer's email
Keep the host’s email address private from attendees.
Custom reply-to email
Set a custom reply-to address for booking confirmations and notifications.
Optimized slots
Suggest the best available times based on availability and scheduling preferences.
(Custom) workflows / booking notifications
Automate (custom) reminders, follow-ups, and notifications around bookings.
Webhooks
Send booking data instantly to your systems when meetings are created, updated, canceled or choose from our other triggers.
Redirect on booking
Send attendees to a custom URL after they complete a booking.
Common schedule
Ensure everyone on this event type uses the same shared availability and scheduling rules.
Insights - analytics
Analyze booking trends, team workload, and event performance over time.
Basic routing
Route bookings to the right person or team using simple rules.
Attribute based routing
Route meetings dynamically using custom fields like region, role, or priority.
Member attributes
Assign attributes to teammates (e.g. location or department) for smarter routing.
Restriction schedule
Limit when specific event types can be booked based on rules or schedules.
Make your organization private
Keep teams and sub-teams private so members can’t see or access each other’s schedules unless permitted.
Blocklist
Block specific emails or domains from booking meetings with you.
Teams
Round-robin scheduling
Distribute bookings across a team automatically based on availability, weights and/or priority rules.
Round-robin groups
Create booking pools of teammates and distribute meetings automatically (e.g. pairing a sales rep and engineer for the same call).
Fixed round-robin scheduling
Book meetings with a fixed host while rotating additional teammates from a selected pool.
Collective events
Schedule meetings where multiple team members attend together.
Managed events
Create admin-controlled event templates with lockable settings and consistent team communications.
Dynamic group links
Create a shared booking link on the fly by combining usernames (e.g. cal.com/john+jane) to generate a collective meeting with shared availability.
Recurring events
Allow attendees to book repeating meetings, such as weekly check-ins or ongoing sessions.
Unlimited sub-teams
Organize your company into multiple sub-teams, each with its own members and booking setup.
Set meeting limits
Buffer before & after events
Automatically add time before or after meetings to prevent back-to-back bookings.
Minimum notice
Require bookings to be made a set amount of time in advance.
Time-slot intervals
Control which time increments are available for bookings (e.g. event length or every 15, 30, or 60 minutes).
Limit booking frequency
Limit how many times an event can be booked per day, week, or month.
Limit total booking duration
Limit the total number of minutes this event can be booked per day, week, or month.
Limit number of upcoming bookings per booker
Control how many future meetings a single person can have scheduled at once.
Limit future bookings
Set how far in advance people can book meetings.
Only show the first slot of each day as available
This will limit your availability for an event type to one slot per day, scheduled at the earliest available time.
Customization
Translate meeting title & description
Offer booking pages and event details in multiple languages.
Custom branding on booking page
Customize brand color, add your logo, and choose between light and dark mode to match your brand.
Remove Cal.com branding
Remove Cal.com logos and references from your booking pages.
Add your own logo
Display your company logo on booking pages and email communications.
Custom sub-domain
Host your booking pages on your Cal.com sub-domain (yourcompany.cal.com.
Data management & security
HIPAA compliance
Support HIPAA requirements for handling protected health information (PHI).
Roles & permissions
Control who can create, manage, and edit scheduling settings across your organization.
SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)
Allow secure login through your identity provider (e.g. Okta, Azure AD).
User provisioning via SCIM
Automatically create, update, and remove user accounts through your identity provider.
Domain wide delegation
Let administrators manage scheduling settings across all users in your company domain.
SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDRP & CCPA compliance check
Meet enterprise security and data protection standards with audited controls and policies.
US & EU hosting
Choose where your data is hosted to meet regional data residency requirements.
Dedicated database
Isolate your organization’s data in a dedicated database environment for enhanced security and performance.
Integrations
Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, Cal Video and 20+ other conferencing tools
Automatically add video meeting links to bookings using your preferred conferencing platform.
Google Calendar, Outlook, Apple Calendar, Zoho Calendar + more
Sync availability and events across all your connected calendars in real time.
Webhooks
Send booking data instantly to your systems when meetings are created, updated, canceled or choose from our other triggers.
Salesforce
Two-way sync meetings and contacts with Salesforce, and route bookings based on CRM data and ownership.
HubSpot
Two-way sync with HubSpot and automatically route meetings to the right rep based on account ownership.
Stripe & Paypal for booking payments
Request payment upfront before someone can complete a booking with you.
Mobile app & browser extensions
Manage bookings on the go and schedule faster directly from your browser or phone.
Zapier & Make
Connect Cal.com to thousands of apps and automate workflows without writing code.
100+ other direct integrations
Choose from a growing library of integrations to tailor Cal.com to your workflow.
Smarter, simpler scheduling
Get started
Talk to sales

On this page
Authentication
1. Create an OAuth client and “Continue with Cal.com”
2. API key
Teams endpoints
Organizations endpoints
Rate limits
Deprecated & Maintenance for existing users only
2. Platform OAuth client credentials
3. Platform Managed user access token
Platform endpoints
Getting Started
Introduction to API v2
Copy page
Introduction to Cal.com API v2 endpoints
Copy page
​
Authentication
The Cal.com API has 3 authentication methods:
OAuth
API key
Platform (Deprecated)
​
1. Create an OAuth client and “Continue with Cal.com”
In order to be listed as an official partner and App in our App Store: cal.com/apps you need to create and get a verified OAuth client.
You can request it here:
https://cal.com/docs/api-reference/v2/oauth
.
​
2. API key
While API keys can be created easily, bear in mind we almost always recommend using OAuth credentials, especially when building integrations or applications with Cal.com.
You can view and manage your API keys in your settings page under the security tab in Cal.com.
API Keys are under Settings > Security
Test mode secret keys have the prefix
cal_
and live mode secret keys have the prefix
cal_live_
.
Your API keys carry many privileges, so be sure to keep them secure! Do not share your secret API keys in publicly accessible areas such as GitHub, client-side code, and so forth.
Authentication to the API is performed via the Authorization header. For example, the request would go something like:
'Authorization': 'Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'
in your request header.
All API requests must be made over HTTPS. Calls made over plain HTTP will fail. API requests without authentication will also fail.
​
Teams endpoints
Teams customers have all the endpoints except the ones prefixed with “Platform” and “Orgs”.
​
Organizations endpoints
Organizations customers have all the endpoints except the ones prefixed with “Platform” and “Teams” and “Orgs / Orgs” because
children organizations are only allowed in the platform plan right now.
​
Rate limits
There are three authentication methods for the API, and each of them has the following rate limits:
API Key - 120 requests per minute. This can be increased to a reasonable amount, such as 200 requests per minute. If you require a higher rate limit, such as 800 requests per minute, it is possible, but it may involve extra charges. To request this, please contact support.
If no authentication method is provided, the default rate limit is 120 requests per minute.
​
Deprecated & Maintenance for existing users only
As of 15th December 2025, we’re currently undergoing a restructuring of our “Platform”-offering. Until further we continue to provide enterprise support for existing customers but no longer offer new signups for any “Platform” plan.
​
2. Platform OAuth client credentials
You need to use OAuth credentials when:
Managing managed users
API reference
Creating OAuth client webhooks
API reference
Refreshing tokens of a managed user
API reference
Teams related endpoints: Managing organization teams
API reference
, adding managed users as members to teams
API reference
, creating team event types
API reference
.
OAuth credentials can be accessed in the platform dashboard
https://app.cal.com/settings/platform
after you have created an OAuth client. Each one has an ID and secret. You then need to pass them as request headers:
x-cal-client-id
- ID of the OAuth client.
x-cal-secret-key
- secret of the OAuth client.
​
3. Platform Managed user access token
After you create a managed user you will receive its access and refresh tokens. The response also includes managed user’s id, so we recommend you to add new properties to your users table calAccessToken, calRefreshToken and calManagedUserId to store this information.
You need to use access token when managing managed user’s:
Schedules
API reference
Event types
API reference
Bookings - some endpoints like creating a booking is public, but some like getting all managed user’s bookings require managed user’s access token
API reference
It is passed as an authorization bearer request header Authorization: Bearer <access-token>.
Validity period: access tokens are valid for 60 minutes and refresh tokens for 1 year, and tokens can be refreshed using the refresh endpoint
API reference
. After refreshing you will receive the new access and refresh tokens that you have to store in your database.
Recovering tokens: if you ever lose managed user’s access or refresh tokens, you can force refresh them using the OAuth client credentials and store them in your database
API reference
.
​
Platform endpoints
Platform customers have the following endpoints available:
Endpoints prefixed with “Platform”.
Endpoints with no prefix e.g “Bookings”, “Event Types”.
If you are at least on the ESSENTIALS plan, then all endpoints prefixed with “Orgs” except “Orgs / Attributes”, “Orgs / Attributes / Options” and “Orgs / Teams / Routing forms / Responses”.
Was this page helpful?
Yes
No
OAuth
⌘
I
x
github
linkedin
Powered by
This documentation is built and hosted on Mintlify, a developer documentation platform
Assistant
Responses are generated using AI and may contain mistakes.

Automate Cal.com workflows with 2000+ app integrations.
Details
fonio.ai
Der KI-Telefonassistent auf Deutsch
Details
Caretta
Realtime Sales AI
Details
Clic
Create, List and Interact with Your Cal.com links and connections easily.
Details
Cal Video
Cal Video is the in-house web-based video conferencing platform powered by Daily.co, which is minimalistic and lightweight, but has most of the features you need.
Details
Default
Google Calendar
Google Calendar is a time management and scheduling service developed by Google. Allows users to create and edit events, with options available for type and time. Available to anyone that has a Gmail account on both mobile and web versions.
Details
Google Meet
Google Meet is Google's web-based video conferencing platform, designed to compete with major conferencing platforms.
Details
Zoom Video
Zoom is a secure and reliable video platform that supports all of your online communication needs. It can provide everything from one on one meetings, chat, phone, webinars, and large-scale online events. Available with both desktop, web, and mobile versions.
Details
Outlook Calendar
Microsoft Office 365 is a suite of apps that helps you stay connected with others and get things done. It includes but is not limited to Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Teams, OneNote and OneDrive. Office 365 allows you to work remotely with others on a team and collaborate in an online environment. Both web versions and desktop/mobile applications are available.
Details
Microsoft 365/Teams (Requires work/school account)
Microsoft Teams is a business communication platform and collaborative workspace included in Microsoft 365. It offers workspace chat and video conferencing, file storage, and application integration. Both web versions and desktop/mobile applications are available. NOTE: MUST HAVE A WORK / SCHOOL ACCOUNT
Details
Stripe
A Saas company a payment processing software, and application programming interfaces for e-commerce websites and mobile applications.
Details
Apple Calendar
Apple calendar runs both the macOS and iOS mobile operating systems. Offering online cloud backup of calendars using Apple's iCloud service, it can sync with Google Calendar and Microsoft Exchange Server. Users can schedule events in their day that include time, location, duration, and extra notes.
Details
Discord
Copy your server invite link and start scheduling calls in Discord! Discord is a VoIP and instant messaging social platform. Users have the ability to communicate with voice calls, video calls, text messaging, media and files in private chats or as part of communities.
Details
WhatsApp
Schedule a chat with your guests or have a WhatsApp Video call.
Details
Paypal
Paypal payment app by Cal.com
Details
Make
From tasks and workflows to apps and systems, build and automate anything in one powerful visual platform.
Details
Google Analytics
Google Analytics is a web analytics service offered by Google that tracks and reports website traffic, currently as a platform inside the Google Marketing Platform brand.
Details
n8n
Automate without limits. The workflow automation platform that doesn't box you in, that you never outgrow
Details
HubSpot CRM
HubSpot is a cloud-based CRM designed to help align sales and marketing teams, foster sales enablement, boost ROI and optimize your inbound marketing strategy to generate more, qualified leads.
Details
Facetime
Facetime makes it super simple for collaborating teams to jump on a video call.
Details
QR Code
Easily generate a QR code for your links to print, share, or embed.
Details
Jitsi Video
Jitsi is a free open-source video conferencing software for web and mobile. Make a call, launch on your own servers, integrate into your app, and more.

Zoho Calendar is an online business calendar that makes scheduling easy for you. You can use it to stay on top of your schedule and also share calendars with your team to keep everyone on the same page.

Meta Pixel

Details
CalDav (Beta)
Caldav is a protocol that allows different clients/servers to access scheduling information on remote servers as well as schedule meetings with other users on the same server or other servers. It extends WebDAV specification and uses iCalendar format for the data.

Zapier

Telegram

GIPHY is your top source for the best & newest GIFs & Animated Stickers online. Find everything from funny GIFs, reaction GIFs, unique GIFs and more.

Supercharge your Call Operations with AI

Your online recording studio. The easiest way to record podcasts and videos in studio quality from anywhere. All from the browser.
Details
Signal
Schedule a chat with your guests or have a Signal Video call.
Details
Fathom
Fathom Analytics provides simple, privacy-focused website analytics. We're a GDPR-compliant, Google Analytics alternative.
Details
Skype
Skype is for connecting with the people that matter most in your life and work. It's built for both one-on-one and group conversations and works wherever you are – via mobile, PC and Alexa. Skype messaging and HD voice and video calling will help you share experiences and get things done with others.
Details
Attio

Details
Raycast
Quickly share your Cal.com meeting links with Raycast
Details
Posthog
PostHog is the all-in-one platform for building better products - with product analytics, feature flags, session recordings, a/b testing, heatmaps, and more.

Alby
Your Bitcoin & Nostr companion for the web. Use Alby to charge Satoshi for your Cal.com meetings.

Element is an open-source communication platform that provides messaging, voice calling, and video conferencing capabilities. It is based on the Matrix protocol, which is a decentralized and federated messaging protocol designed to enable secure and interoperable communication across different platforms and services."

The joyful productivity app

Zoho CRM is a cloud-based application designed to help your salespeople sell smarter and faster by centralizing customer information, logging their interactions with your company, and automating many of the tasks salespeople do every day

Bigin easily transforms your day-to-day customer processes into actionable pipelines. From qualifying leads to closing deals to managing important after-sales operations—Bigin connects your different teams to work together so that you can offer the best possible experience to your customers. Say goodbye to missing follow-ups, manual data entry, lack of team communication, and information silos.

analysis.name: Cal.com
analysis.tagline: The better way to schedule your meetings
analysis.value_proposition: A fully customizable, open-source scheduling platform for individuals, businesses, and developers building scheduling into their own products.
analysis.ideal_customer: Individuals, small teams, and developer-led companies who want an open, customizable Calendly alternative — plus enterprises needing embeddable scheduling infrastructure.

features.name: unlimited event types
features.description: Create unlimited booking templates for different meeting types.
features.evidence_quote: Unlimited event types & calendars

features.name: unlimited calendar connections
features.description: Connect multiple calendars to sync availability and prevent double bookings.
features.evidence_quote: Connect multiple calendars to sync availability and prevent double bookings.

features.name: unlimited meetings
features.description: Accept unlimited bookings without restrictions on number of meetings.
features.evidence_quote: Accept unlimited bookings without restrictions on the number of meetings.

features.name: multiple duration options
features.description: Offer different meeting lengths for the same event type (15/30/60 min).
features.evidence_quote: Offer different meeting lengths for the same event type (e.g. 15, 30, or 60 mins)

features.name: custom meeting locations
features.description: Define where meetings take place — Zoom, Meet, phone, or in person.
features.evidence_quote: Define where meetings take place, such as Zoom, Google Meet, phone, or in person.

features.name: availability rules
features.description: Set specific days, hours, and availability rules per event type.
features.evidence_quote: Set specific days, hours, and availability rules for each event type.

features.name: timezone detection
features.description: Automatically adjust for attendee timezones and prevent conflicts.
features.evidence_quote: Automatically adjust for attendee timezones and prevent scheduling conflicts.

features.name: meeting cancellation
features.description: Cancel meetings via secure link for organizer and attendees.
features.evidence_quote: Allow organizer and attendees to cancel meetings using a secure link.

features.name: meeting rescheduling
features.description: Reschedule without back-and-forth emails.
features.evidence_quote: Allow organizer and attendees to reschedule without back-and-forth emails.

features.description: Transfer a scheduled meeting to another team member.
features.evidence_quote: Transfer a scheduled meeting to another team member when needed.

features.name: buffers and minimum notice
features.description: Add buffers around events and set daily/weekly/monthly booking limits.
features.evidence_quote: Set daily, weekly or monthly limits and add buffers around your events

features.name: custom booking link
features.description: Short, memorable custom URL like cal.com/rajiv.
features.evidence_quote: Stand out with a custom booking link

features.name: booking calendar & list view
features.description: View and manage upcoming bookings in calendar or list format.
features.evidence_quote: View and manage all your upcoming bookings in a calendar or list format.

features.name: custom booking questions
features.description: Customize the questions attendees answer when booking.
features.evidence_quote: Customize the questions attendees answer when booking.

features.name: require confirmation
features.description: Review and approve booking requests before they are scheduled.
features.evidence_quote: Review and approve booking requests before the meeting is officially scheduled.

features.name: email verification
features.description: Ensure attendees confirm their email before booking is finalized.
features.evidence_quote: Ensure attendees confirm their email before the booking is finalized.

features.name: private links
features.description: Invite-only booking links that aren't publicly discoverable.
features.evidence_quote: Create invite-only booking links that aren't publicly discoverable.

features.name: seats per booking
features.description: Allow multiple people to book the same time slot for group sessions.
features.evidence_quote: Allow multiple people to book the same time slot for group sessions or classes.

features.name: hide organizer email
features.description: Keep host's email address private from attendees.
features.evidence_quote: Keep the host's email address private from attendees.

features.name: custom reply-to email
features.description: Set a custom reply-to for booking confirmations.
features.evidence_quote: Set a custom reply-to address for booking confirmations and notifications.

features.name: optimized slots
features.description: Suggest best available times based on scheduling preferences.
features.evidence_quote: Suggest the best available times based on availability and scheduling preferences.

features.name: workflows
features.description: Automate custom reminders, follow-ups, and notifications.
features.evidence_quote: Automate (custom) reminders, follow-ups, and notifications around bookings.

features.name: webhooks
features.description: Send booking data to your systems on create/update/cancel triggers.
features.evidence_quote: Send booking data instantly to your systems when meetings are created, updated, canceled

features.name: redirect on booking
features.description: Send attendees to a custom URL after completing a booking.
features.evidence_quote: Send attendees to a custom URL after they complete a booking.

features.name: sms notifications
features.description: Email & SMS notifications for bookings.
features.evidence_quote: Email & SMS notifications

features.name: automated meeting reminders
features.description: SMS/email reminders and automated follow-ups.
features.evidence_quote: Reduce no-shows with automated meeting reminders

differentiators: Developer-first with API v2, Atoms SDK, and self-hosting
Free tier is genuinely unlimited (event types, calendars, meetings)
Two-way Salesforce & HubSpot sync included on free tier
Embeddable platform for building scheduling into other products (managed users)
Cal.ai + MCP server for AI-agent scheduling
Cal Video built-in — no third-party video required

recent_ships.what: Platform plan restructuring — no new Platform signups; existing customers still supported
recent_ships.source: As of 15th December 2025, we're currently undergoing a restructuring of our "Platform"-offering.

recent_ships.what: MCP server and AI agents endpoints added to API v2

praised: "More elegant than Calendly, more open than SavvyCal, Cal.com works and it feels just right." — Flo Merian, Mintlify
"I just migrated from Calendly to Cal.com." — Kent C. Dodds
"Just gave it a go and it's definitely the easiest meeting I've ever scheduled!" — Aria Minaei, Theatre.JS
"At Navi, protecting personal health information is a non-negotiable, so choosing Cal.com… just makes sense." — Micah Friedland
Open-source origins and Calendly-alternative positioning consistently praised on HN
complaints: Backlash on HN over Cal.com moving core codebase to closed source ("Cal.com is going closed source" — 391↑/317c)

free_tier.limits: 1 user; unlimited event types, calendars, and meetings

tiers.name: Individuals (Free)
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 user, unlimited event types/calendars, 100+ apps, mobile, Stripe/PayPal, Calendly import

tiers.name: Teams
tiers.headline_inclusions: Round-robin, managed/collective events, recurring, routing forms, analytics, custom APIs

tiers.name: Organizations
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited sub-teams, SAML SSO/SCIM, SOC 2/HIPAA/ISO 27001, subdomain, RBAC

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: Dedicated onboarding, SLA, HRIS integrations, priority Slack support, dedicated database

pricing.notes: Advertised prices are annual (Save 25% badge on Teams & Organizations); 14-day free trial on Teams and Organizations. Platform plan is in restructuring as of Dec 2025 — no new signups.
